children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: There is established in the Department of Homeland Security a board to be known as the “Transportation Security Oversight Board”.

children.label: A
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security, or the Secretary’s designee.

children.label: B
children.text: The Secretary of Transportation, or the Secretary’s designee.

children.label: C
children.text: The Attorney General, or the Attorney General’s designee.

children.label: D
children.text: The Secretary of Defense, or the Secretary’s designee.

children.label: E
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury, or the Secretary’s designee.

children.label: F
children.text: The Director of National Intelligence, or the Director’s designee.

children.label: G
children.text: One member appointed by the President to represent the National Security Council.

children.heading: .—Chairperson
children.label: 2 .—Chairperson
children.text: The Chairperson of the Board shall be the Secretary of Homeland Security.

review and ratify or disapprove any regulation or security directive issued by the Administrator of the Transportation Security Administration under section 114()(2) within 30 days after the date of issuance of such regulation or directive;l
facilitate the coordination of intelligence, security, and law enforcement activities affecting transportation;
facilitate the sharing of intelligence, security, and law enforcement information affecting transportation among Federal agencies and with carriers and other transportation providers as appropriate;
explore the technical feasibility of developing a common database of individuals who may pose a threat to transportation or national security;
make recommendations to the Administrator regarding matters reviewed under paragraph (5).

children.heading: .—Quarterly Meetings
children.label: d .—Quarterly Meetings
children.text: The Board shall meet at least quarterly.

children.heading: .—Consideration of Security Information
children.label: e .—Consideration of Security Information
children.text: A majority of the Board may vote to close a meeting of the Board to the public, except that meetings shall be closed to the public whenever classified, sensitive security information, or information protected in accordance with section 40119(b), will be discussed.12So in original. The word “information” probably should be inserted.1See References in Text note below.2
